codeReview.summary: The implementation provides a working summary service and API endpoint, but does not fulfill VAL-SESSION-006 which requires that 'after session ends, AI generates a summary'. The summary must be triggered manually via a separate API call - it is not automatically generated when a session ends.

issues.description: The /summarize endpoint exists but is not wired into the session-end flow. VAL-SESSION-006 requires that 'after session ends, AI generates a summary' - this should happen automatically when end_session is called, not as a separate manual step.
issues.evidence: POST /api/sessions/{session_id}/summarize is a separate endpoint that must be called independently. The end_session endpoint (line 223) does not call summary_service.generate_and_store_summary().

issues.description: The frontend end_session method calls api_client.end_session() but does not subsequently call the summarize endpoint. For VAL-SESSION-006 to be fulfilled, the UI should trigger summary generation after session ends.
issues.evidence: session_page.py end_session method: only calls end_session API, no subsequent call to generate summary

issues.description: The feature description says 'AI-powered session summary generation' but the implementation is template-based keyword extraction, not LLM-powered. The code acknowledges this in comments: 'The actual AI-powered analysis would be done via LLM in a future enhancement.'
issues.evidence: SummaryService.generate_summary() uses simple string matching against keywords like 'hello', 'feel', 'thank'. It does not use any LLM.

issues.description: The generate_summary endpoint could be more explicit about when and how it should be called. OpenAPI docs mention 'on-demand' but the feature expects it to be automatic.
issues.evidence: Docstring says 'Generate an AI-powered summary for a session' but doesn't clarify it should be called automatically after end_session

sharedStateObservations.observation: The implementation follows existing backend patterns correctly. SummaryService mirrors SessionService structure. Tests follow existing test patterns. No convention gaps found.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: All tests pass, ruff and mypy checks pass, code follows Python style from AGENTS.md

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker followed the backend-worker skill procedure correctly. The skill's work procedure was followed. However, the skill doesn't explicitly require automatic end-of-session triggering for summary generation - this may be a gap between the feature's expectedBehavior and the skill's documented procedure.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows standard procedure followed: implement, test, verify, commit

summary: The session-summary-generation feature provides a functional summary service and API endpoint, but fails VAL-SESSION-006 because summary generation is not automatically triggered when a session ends. The feature description states 'After session ends, AI generates a summary' - this automatic behavior is missing. Users must manually call /api/sessions/{session_id}/summarize after ending a session. The implementation correctly handles edge cases (empty sessions, non-existent sessions, authorization) and all tests pass, but the core requirement of automatic post-session summary generation is not fulfilled.
